title: Translation accuracy against the agreed glossary
question: Should the payee (translator Mara Volkova) be paid in full under the linked terms, given the client's dispute over technical-term accuracy?

evidence.label: Signed services agreement and statement of work (5,000-word technical manual, German to English)
Agreed terminology glossary (142 technical terms, version dated 2026-04-10)
Delivered translation with the client's marked accuracy objections
Term-by-term comparison: delivered renderings versus the glossary

terms: Northwind Localization Ltd. (client) engaged Mara Volkova (payee) to translate a 5,000-word technical manual from German into English for a flat fee of 950 euros, held in escrow. The contract required that all 142 terms in the agreed glossary dated 2026-04-10 be rendered exactly as specified, with no more than 3 deviations permitted across the document. Payment is due in full if the delivered translation meets the word count and the glossary-conformance limit; the client may withhold only on glossary deviations, not on stylistic preferences outside the glossary.
